results.pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_users FROM identity.users

SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_product_count
FROM catalog.products
WHERE status = 'active'
SELECT code FROM geo.currencies

SELECT COUNT(*) AS refunded_orders_count
FROM sales.returns
WHERE status = 'refunded'

SELECT AVG(grand_total) AS average_grand_total
FROM sales.orders

SELECT COUNT(*) AS urgent_ticket_count
FROM support.tickets
WHERE priority = 'urgent'

SELECT DISTINCT from_status AS order_status FROM sales.order_status_history
UNION
SELECT DISTINCT to_status AS order_status FROM sales.order_status_history

SELECT SUM(billing.payments.amount) AS total_captured_payments
FROM billing.payments
WHERE billing.payments.status = 'captured'
SELECT 'insufficient schema: missing archived product information' AS error

SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_subscriptions
FROM billing.subscriptions
WHERE status = 'active'

SELECT COUNT(*) AS country_count
FROM geo.countries
WHERE continent = 'Europe'

SELECT AVG(employee_count) AS average_employee_count
FROM crm.accounts
WHERE is_active = true
SELECT 'insufficient schema: missing sign-up information' AS error

SELECT COUNT(*) AS open_tickets_count
FROM support.tickets
WHERE status <> 'closed'

SELECT COUNT(*) AS overdue_invoices_count
FROM billing.invoices
WHERE due_at < NOW() AND status != 'paid'

SELECT 
    catalog.products.id AS product_id,
    catalog.products.name AS product_name,
    catalog.brands.name AS brand_name
FROM 
    catalog.products
JOIN 
    catalog.brands ON catalog.products.brand_id = catalog.brands.id

SELECT 
    catalog.product_reviews.id AS review_id,
    catalog.product_reviews.rating,
    catalog.product_reviews.title,
    catalog.product_reviews.body,
    catalog.products.name AS product_name
FROM 
    catalog.product_reviews
JOIN 
    catalog.products ON catalog.product_reviews.product_id = catalog.products.id

SELECT 
    billing.subscriptions.id AS subscription_id,
    billing.plans.name AS plan_name
FROM 
    billing.subscriptions
JOIN 
    billing.plans ON billing.subscriptions.plan_id = billing.plans.id

SELECT sales.orders.id AS order_id, identity.users.email AS customer_email
FROM sales.orders
JOIN identity.users ON sales.orders.user_id = identity.users.id

SELECT p.id, p.name
FROM catalog.products p
LEFT JOIN catalog.product_reviews r ON p.id = r.product_id
WHERE r.id IS NULL

SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_subscribers
FROM billing.subscriptions AS s
JOIN billing.plans AS p ON s.plan_id = p.id
WHERE s.status = 'active' AND p.code = 'pro'

SELECT 
    o.currency_code,
    SUM(o.grand_total) AS total_revenue
FROM 
    sales.orders o
GROUP BY 
    o.currency_code

SELECT tenant_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM sales.orders
GROUP BY tenant_id

SELECT p.id, p.name, AVG(pr.rating) AS average_rating
FROM catalog.products p
JOIN catalog.product_reviews pr ON p.id = pr.product_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.name
HAVING AVG(pr.rating) > 4

SELECT identity.users.id AS user_id, identity.users.email AS user_email, COUNT(sales.orders.id) AS order_count
FROM identity.users
JOIN sales.orders ON identity.users.id = sales.orders.user_id
GROUP BY identity.users.id
HAVING COUNT(sales.orders.id) > 3

SELECT 
    c.name AS shipping_country,
    SUM(o.grand_total) AS total_order_revenue
FROM 
    sales.orders o
JOIN 
    geo.addresses a ON o.shipping_address_id = a.id
JOIN 
    geo.countries c ON a.country_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
    c.name
ORDER BY 
    total_order_revenue DESC

SELECT 
    catalog.products.id AS product_id,
    catalog.products.name AS product_name,
    SUM(sales.order_items.quantity) AS total_units_sold
FROM 
    sales.order_items
JOIN 
    sales.orders ON sales.order_items.order_id = sales.orders.id
JOIN 
    catalog.product_variants ON sales.order_items.product_variant_id = catalog.product_variants.id
JOIN 
    catalog.products ON catalog.product_variants.product_id = catalog.products.id
GROUP BY 
    catalog.products.id, catalog.products.name
ORDER BY 
    total_units_sold DESC
LIMIT 5

SELECT DISTINCT crm.contacts.primary_email
FROM billing.payment_refunds
JOIN billing.payments ON billing.payment_refunds.payment_id = billing.payments.id
JOIN crm.contacts ON billing.payments.tenant_id = crm.contacts.tenant_id
WHERE crm.contacts.primary_email IS NOT NULL

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT identity.users.id) AS user_count
FROM identity.users
JOIN geo.addresses ON identity.users.id = geo.addresses.user_id
JOIN geo.countries ON geo.addresses.country_id = geo.countries.id
WHERE geo.countries.name = 'Germany'

SELECT bp.code AS plan_code, COUNT(bs.id) AS active_subscription_count
FROM billing.plans bp
LEFT JOIN billing.subscriptions bs ON bp.id = bs.plan_id AND bs.status = 'active'
GROUP BY bp.code

SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', placed_at) AS month,
    COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM 
    sales.orders
WHERE 
    placed_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '12 months'
GROUP BY 
    month
ORDER BY 
    month

SELECT 
    identity.tenants.id AS tenant_id,
    identity.tenants.name AS tenant_name,
    SUM(sales.orders.grand_total) AS total_revenue
FROM 
    identity.tenants
JOIN 
    sales.orders ON identity.tenants.id = sales.orders.tenant_id
GROUP BY 
    identity.tenants.id, identity.tenants.name
ORDER BY 
    total_revenue DESC

SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (resolved_at - created_at)) / 86400) AS average_days_to_resolve
FROM support.tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL

SELECT COUNT(o.id) AS order_count
FROM sales.orders o
JOIN identity.users u ON o.user_id = u.id AND o.tenant_id = u.tenant_id

SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(pc.product_id) AS product_count
FROM catalog.categories c
JOIN catalog.product_categories pc ON c.id = pc.category_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
ORDER BY product_count DESC
LIMIT 3

SELECT SUM(grand_total) AS total_revenue_eur
FROM sales.orders
WHERE currency_code = 'EUR'

SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sales.orders WHERE status = 'cancelled')::numeric / COUNT(*) AS cancelled_order_fraction
FROM 
    sales.orders

SELECT *
FROM crm.accounts
WHERE employee_count > 100

SELECT 
    bp.name AS plan_name,
    AVG(o.grand_total) AS average_order_value
FROM 
    billing.subscriptions s
JOIN 
    billing.plans bp ON s.plan_id = bp.id
JOIN 
    sales.orders o ON s.user_id = o.user_id
WHERE 
    o.status = 'completed' -- Assuming we only want completed orders
GROUP BY 
    bp.name

SELECT 
    status, 
    COUNT(*) AS paid_invoices_count
FROM 
    billing.invoices
WHERE 
    status = 'paid'
GROUP BY 
    status
